nodes.label: Lenia — Biology of Artificial Life
nodes.summary: Founding paper. Continuous CA with bell kernel + polynomial growth; 400+ self-organized solitons.

nodes.label: Lenia and Expanded Universe
nodes.summary: Extends Lenia to higher dimensions, multiple channels, richer kernels.

nodes.label: Automating the Search for Artificial Life with Foundation Models (ASAL)
nodes.authors: Kumar, Lu, Kirsch, Tang, Stanley, Isola, Ha (Sakana AI)
nodes.summary: CLIP-judge-based ALife search. 3 objectives × 3 algorithms × 7 substrates including Lenia.

nodes.label: Glaberish: generalizing Lenia composition rules
nodes.summary: Decouples kernel from growth; new families outside Lenia's search space.

nodes.label: Existence of Life in Lenia
nodes.summary: Soliton stability and parameter-space invariance — conditions for creature persistence under perturbation.

nodes.label: Orbium (canonical Lenia glider)
nodes.summary: Continuous analog of Game-of-Life glider. R=13, T=10, μ=0.15, σ=0.015, polynomial K and G. Reproduced in lenia-baseline.

nodes.label: Sep-CMA-ES
nodes.summary: Separable Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolution Strategy — ASAL's optimizer for supervised + open-endedness objectives in continuous rule space.

nodes.label: CLIP embedding as ALife judge
nodes.summary: ASAL's core idea: foundation-model embedding turns qualitative 'is this lifelike?' into a differentiable scalar via cosine sim to text prompts or temporal-novelty.

nodes.label: Does CLIP-as-judge get gamed by adversarial textures?
nodes.summary: Open question: ASAL's open-endedness metric maximizes embedding diversity. If a simulation produces visually high-entropy but biologically uninteresting patterns, CLIP may reward it. Worth a controlled test.

nodes.label: Foundation-model-free lifelikeness metric
nodes.summary: Could a metric built from physical invariants (mass quasi-conservation + locomotion + soliton persistence + bilateral structure) replace CLIP and still rediscover Orbium-class creatures? Cheaper, interpretable, less gameable.

nodes.label: lenia-baseline (this orbit)
nodes.summary: Reproduces Orbium with Chan parameters; FFT toroidal convolution; mass + COM tracking. Substrate for downstream search experiments.

nodes.label: lenia-zoo (this orbit)
nodes.summary: Six creatures × five diagnostics. Validates a foundation-model-free lifelikeness vector (mass_cv, speed, footprint, symmetry, persistent) on Orbium uni/bi/phantasma, Gyrorbium, Synorbium, Vagorbium.

nodes.label: Lenia behavior modes (translate / rotate / undulate)
nodes.summary: Empirically observed in lenia-zoo: speed + footprint + symmetry separates Chan's species cleanly into translators (~6 px/u), rotators (Gyrorbium, COM-loop), and large undulators (Vagorbium, 614-px footprint).

nodes.label: Generalize bilateral symmetry to full D_n group
nodes.summary: Current implementation only checks H/V reflection, undersells D4-symmetric creatures (Synorbium scores 0.31). Need rotation-aware best-axis search before freezing eval.

nodes.label: Reward-hacking surface of the diagnostic vector
nodes.summary: Static blob trivially passes mass_cv≈0, footprint>0, symmetry→1, persistent=True. Need a temporal-complexity leg (e.g., variance of area_t and sym_t post-transient) to rule out trivial winners.

edges.label: uses Lenia as one of 7 substrates

meta.intent_statement: Build a foundation-model-free lifelikeness metric for ASAL-style Lenia search; prototype 5-dim vector cleanly separates Chan's translator/rotator/undulator behavior modes on a six-creature zoo; gaps (D_n symmetry, reward-hacking surface) identified for /init.
